title: Incursa.Quic Standard Client TLS Validation Verification
verification_method: Execution, inspection, and regression evidence for the standard BCL-shaped client-validation path on the existing client carrier.
preconditions: The incumbent API and CRT client-policy artifacts for `REQ-QUIC-API-0012` and `REQ-QUIC-CRT-0123` exist.
The standard client-validation path is captured on the existing `SslClientAuthenticationOptions` carrier.
The exact-match `PeerCertificatePolicy` floor remains separate and available for regression comparison.
scope: This artifact records the proof burden for the mainstream public client-validation mode on the existing `SslClientAuthenticationOptions` carrier. In the current slice it verifies standard BCL-shaped `TargetHost`, `CertificateChainPolicy`, revocation delegation, and callback override behavior, while keeping the exact-pinning floor on `QuicPeerCertificatePolicy` separate. It does not claim broader client-auth, `IsSupported` widening, transfer changes, retry changes, 0-RTT, or key update.
expected_result: The canonical trace shows the standard client-validation path on the existing `SslClientAuthenticationOptions` carrier, positive validation succeeds when the target host and trust policy match, trust mismatch fails closed, hostname mismatch fails closed when that semantic is in scope, mixed-mode configuration is rejected, and the exact-match `PeerCertificatePolicy` floor remains unchanged.
procedure: Inspect `SPEC-QUIC-API`, `ARC-QUIC-API-0004`, `WI-QUIC-API-0004`, `VER-QUIC-API-0004`, and `REQUIREMENT-GAPS.md` to confirm the standard BCL-shaped path is traced on the existing client carrier and kept separate from the exact-pinning floor.

Inspect the current source and requirement-home guards to confirm `QuicClientConnectionOptionsValidator` captures `TargetHost` and `CertificateChainPolicy` for the standard path and that `REQ-QUIC-API-0012` / `REQ-QUIC-CRT-0123` still own the landed exact-match path.
Run the new requirement-home tests for standard validation success, trust mismatch, hostname mismatch, and mixed-mode rejection.
Keep the incumbent `REQ-QUIC-API-0005`, `REQ-QUIC-API-0012`, and `REQ-QUIC-CRT-0123` coverage green so the standard path and exact-match floor do not regress.
status_summary: This verification artifact now records the standard BCL-shaped client-validation proof obligation on the existing carrier while preserving the exact-match floor as the separate advanced path.
